creator: ant_mail@inbox.ru
text:
May I ask you about backup best practice for UFS?

I'm using dump(8) as proposed in handbook but have the following issues:

1. It's take a long time... But the main problem is it consume a lot of disk IO and slowing users making them angry. I tried to renice it:

nice -n 20 dump -0Lau -b 64 -C 64 -f - /data/office 

but it seems do nothing with IO priority. User expireinces become bad while dumping and I don't know what to do with it.

2. I'm packing dump output via compressor (zstd) to reduce network transfer time and storage space. Am I understand right that to recover (some) files I have to unpack dump files at every dump level and only after that use restore(8)? For hundreds gigabytes of dumps this become a problem to have a free space equivalent to production system and to wait uncompressing. Am I missing some better practice?
